mega_tasks.title: Luxury Shopify hamper line — 13-hamper editorial retouch: per-frame geometry + masked tonal grade, selectively-enhanced embossed leather badge, warmed wicker, two-stage depth blur, shared quiet-luxury preset, cut-out onto a light-grey studio backdrop with a realistic contact shadow, Shopify exports
mega_tasks.source: Upwork db-4478 'Luxury Product Photo Editor Needed – Consistent Premium Shopify Hamper Images (13 Photos)'
mega_tasks.one_line_ask: Edit 13 luxury gifting-hamper photos to one quiet-luxury (Fortnum & Mason / Daylesford) look — geometry-correct each frame, build a masked tonal grade (warm wicker, opened shadows, restrained colour), selectively enhance the embossed leather front badge, add two-stage editorial depth blur, lock a single shared Lightroom preset across the set, then cut every hamper out onto a clean light-grey (#E8E8E8) website backdrop with a realistic contact shadow and export Shopify-ready squares.
deliverables: 13 high-resolution edited JPGs sharing ONE quiet-luxury recipe (matched exposure, white balance, opened shadows, restrained saturation, locked final preset)
Every hamper cut out cleanly and composited onto a light-grey (#E8E8E8) website backdrop with a realistic soft contact shadow

Embossed leather front-centre badge selectively enhanced via a prompt-mask (local exposure lift + local highlight shaping + local dark-portion deepening) so the emboss reads premium without looking AI-processed
Wicker selectively warmed and its yellow saturation lifted under a prompt-mask so only the basket warms, not the ribbons or food
One hero hamper with the fabric/material beneath it removed so the product sits clean on the backdrop
Two-stage editorial depth: whole-frame lens-blur softening on the un-cut lifestyle frames + subject-preserving gaussian background blur on the catalogue frames
Web-ready 2048×2048 Shopify square exports plus the saved, reusable edit recipe applied across the full set

workflow.note: Open an upload session for the raw 13-hamper set + the hero fabric-base frame.
workflow.consumes: input: 13 raw hamper JPGs + 1 hero fabric-base frame

workflow.note: Commit the uploads; returns fetchable presigned asset URLs used as imageURI for the whole chain.
Inline-preview each finalized upload to confirm the right frame/orientation before grading.
Level each handheld frame (basket lip / table edge horizon).
Trim the straightened frame tight to the hamper bounds, killing dead studio floor.
Establish a neutral tonal baseline so every frame starts from the same place before manual grading.
Match global exposure to the agreed set key value (consistency across all 13).
Pull white balance to the warm quiet-luxury target; neutralise the mixed shoot lighting.
Recover blown bright wicker / lid highlights so texture survives.
Tone down the broader bright areas (lid, pale tissue) without crushing highlights — separate control from step 9.
Lift the shadows inside the basket weave to open editorial depth (positive=deepen / negative=lift; here lift).
Set the gentle quiet-luxury contrast curve over the corrected tonal range.
Pull overall saturation back for restraint (vibrance protects skin/food, saturation overall down).
Prompt-mask 'the woven wicker basket body of the hamper' — produces a wicker-only mask.
workflow.consumes: step13 graded image (mask is computed on it)

workflow.note: MASKED warm-up: apply a local temperature push to the wicker ONLY via step14 maskURI, so ribbons/food stay neutral.
workflow.consumes: step13 graded image + step14 wicker mask

workflow.note: MASKED yellow lift: boost 'yellow' saturation under the wicker mask so the basket glows honey-toned, nothing else.
workflow.consumes: step15 wicker-warmed output + step14 wicker mask

workflow.note: Prompt-mask 'the embossed leather badge on the front centre of the hamper' — tight badge mask.
MASKED local lift on the badge only (step17 mask) so the emboss brightens without touching the rest.
MASKED highlight shaping on the badge (step17 mask) to catch the raised leather edges.
workflow.consumes: step18 badge-lifted output + step17 badge mask

workflow.note: MASKED deepen of the badge's recessed channels (step17 mask, positive amount) to make the emboss read 3-D.
MASKED hue/lum nudge on the badge leather (step17 mask) to land the rich tan without an orange cast.
List the 80+ Lightroom presets to pick the exact display name for the shared final look.
Apply ONE shared preset (e.g. a Creative warm split-tone) as the single, non-stacked final colour pass that locks the set look.
workflow.consumes: step21 fully-graded image + step22 chosen preset name

workflow.note: LIFESTYLE FRAMES ONLY: whole-frame lens blur for soft editorial bokeh on the un-cut styled scenes (lens_blur has no mask = whole image).
CATALOGUE FRAMES: auto-detect the hamper as subject — subject mask for both the bg-blur and the cutout.
Subject-preserving background blur (blurTarget:'background') to settle the catalogue backdrop before the clean cut — keeps the hamper crisp.
workflow.consumes: step23 preset-locked frame (subject kept via blurTarget)

workflow.note: HERO FRAME: prompt-mask 'the draped fabric / material underneath the hamper' so it can be removed.
Fill the fabric mask (step27) with the light-grey backdrop colour (custom RGB 232,232,232) so the hero product sits clean — removes the base material.
workflow.consumes: step26 hero frame + step27 fabric mask

workflow.note: Flip the subject mask (step25) to a background mask for the studio backdrop fill.
Fill the inverted (background) mask with light-grey #E8E8E8 (RGB 232,232,232) — the clean Shopify studio backdrop behind the hamper.
workflow.consumes: step28 cleaned frame + step29 background mask

workflow.note: Produce a transparent cutout PNG of the hamper (clean edges) used to composite the realistic contact shadow locally.
Export each composited hamper to the Shopify 2048×2048 square at web resolution.
Preview the final 2048 square exports to QA the set for consistency before delivery.

LOCAL (PIL): drop the step31 transparent cutout onto the #E8E8E8 backdrop and paint a soft realistic contact shadow (Gaussian-feathered ellipse) under each hamper — the connector cuts/grades elements but can't headlessly composite a hand-painted shadow.

iterative_chain: masked-warm.out + wicker mask -> image_adjust_single_color_saturation.in (masked yellow lift)
masked-badge-dark.out + badge mask -> image_adjust_hsl.in (masked leather hue)
badge-final.out -> image_apply_preset.in (single shared look; name from image_list_presets)
fabric mask + hero -> image_fill_area.in (grey over fabric)

input_assets.what: Raw luxury hamper catalogue + lifestyle photos (mixed shoot lighting, wicker, embossed leather front badge) — the 13-image set
Hero hamper frame with draped fabric/material underneath that must be removed before compositing
Brand reference look-board (Fortnum & Mason / Daylesford quiet-luxury aesthetic) anchoring the target grade
Light-grey website background swatch + #E8E8E8 hex spec the cutouts must sit on
Set-grade recipe sheet (target exposure key, WB, shadow-lift and preset name) as a small JSON the editor follows for consistency
Shopify image-spec CSV (filename, SKU, target square px, max file weight) driving the export/QA pass

mega_tasks.title: Real-estate weekly listing stills package — 6 scenes from 5-bracket RAW: HDR-style tonal blend, straightened verticals, window-pulls, one shared gallery preset, print + web JPEGs, plus Illustrator-merged 'just-listed' rider labels
mega_tasks.source: Upwork db-6004 'Real Estate Photo Editor Needed for Bracketed RAW Interior & Exterior Images' (anchored; bracket-blend tonal pipeline)
mega_tasks.one_line_ask: Take 5 bracketed exposures per scene (6 scenes), blend to a natural HDR look via fine tonal grading, straighten verticals/correct perspective, do window pulls, open interiors while keeping clean whites, apply one consistent gallery look, deliver print + web JPEGs, and data-merge a small batch of Illustrator 'just-listed' rider labels from the listing sheet.
deliverables: 6 final natural-HDR scene stills with balanced exposure, recovered window highlights, opened shadows, clean whites and correct perspective
Straight verticals / corrected geometry on every interior and exterior frame
A single shared gallery 'look' (one preset) applied across all 6 so the set is cohesive
Print-ready high-resolution JPEG export of every scene
Web/portal-optimized JPEG export of every scene
A batch of Illustrator-merged 'just-listed' rider/yard-sign labels (address, beds/baths/sqft, agent) rendered from the listing CSV
iterative_chain: asset_finalize_file_upload.out(brightest+darkest bracket pair, per scene) -> image_auto_straighten.in (level + vertical upright)
image_auto_straighten.out -> image_crop_to_bounds.in (trim crooked edges after upright)
image_apply_auto_tone.out -> image_adjust_exposure.in (set mid exposure between brackets)
image_adjust_exposure.out -> image_adjust_highlights.in (window pull — recover blown windows)
image_adjust_color_temperature.out -> image_adjust_hsl.in (calm down over-green lawns / over-blue skies)
image_adjust_hsl.out -> image_apply_preset.in (one shared gallery look; name from image_list_presets)
image_apply_preset.out -> image_crop_and_resize.in (export print hi-res JPEG and web/portal JPEG)
font_recommend.out(label type) -> document_merge_data_vector.in (rider label .ai + listing CSV)
document_merge_data_vector.out(per-row .ai) -> document_render_vector.in (export rider label PNG/PDF set)

input_assets.what: 5-bracket RAW exposures per interior/exterior scene, 6 scenes (we generate the merged-base JPEG per bracket pair the connector grades) — 5x6 frames
Listing fact-sheet CSV (address, beds, baths, sqft, price, agent name/phone) driving the Illustrator rider labels
Illustrator (.ai) 'just-listed' rider / yard-sign label template with variable fields
Agency look reference (target natural-HDR gallery style) to lock the shared preset

mega_tasks.title: Reality-show key-art rebuild — replace AI areas of an approved poster with masked, licensed Adobe Stock photography, depth-graded and re-lit, expanded to 24x36 bleed, delivered print PDF + web JPG
mega_tasks.source: Freelancer db-1847 'PHOTOGRAPHY MASKING/BLENDING Realistic Movie Poster Recreation'
mega_tasks.one_line_ask: Rebuild an approved AI reality-show poster so it looks photographed: source matching real photos from Adobe Stock, mask/cut them in to replace every AI region, blend with believable depth, lighting and shadow, expand the canvas to 24x36 full-bleed, and deliver a print-ready PDF plus a flattened web JPG.
deliverables: Licensed Adobe Stock photography that matches the approved poster's subjects (cast stand-in, sky, environment texture)
Each stock element masked/cut out and composited to replace the corresponding AI region
Depth and focus blended so foreground subjects pop and the background falls off naturally (lens/gaussian blur on background plate)
Lighting/colour matched across all merged layers so the scene reads as one lit set (no AI artifacts at 200% zoom)
Canvas expanded to full 24x36 inch bleed at print spec
Print-ready PDF + flattened web JPG, with a note on stock used
iterative_chain: image_select_subject.out(mask) -> image_remove_background.in (clean cutout to drop over AI figure)
image_select_by_prompt.out(sky mask) -> image_invert_selection.in (select the foreground plate)
image_invert_selection.out(mask) -> image_apply_gaussian_blur.in (soften background plate, background target)
image_apply_lens_blur.out -> image_adjust_exposure.in (match composite exposure to poster mood)
image_adjust_hsl.out -> image_generative_expand.in (extend short axis to 24x36 bleed)

input_assets.what: Approved AI key-art poster supplied as flattened JPG and layered PSD reference (composition/typography locked)
Print spec sheet (24x36in, 300dpi, CMYK) + web-version size (text/data spec)
Adobe Stock photo candidates searched+licensed to replace each AI region (cast stand-in, sky, environment/texture)
Mood/lighting reference frame the client will check the final blend against

mega_tasks.title: Tech-house single release campaign — duotone/halftone+grain streaming cover, selective-colour graded artist promo photos, vectorized logo lockup, and Illustrator-merged tour-date merch cards
mega_tasks.source: Freelancer 'Tech House Single Cover' (verbatim brief, budget $30-250, skills Album Design/Illustrator/Photoshop) composed with Upwork album-art-package brief (id 1610320189970300928: album cover + social banner + promo image + promo video) per scraped pool — FX-heavy release-art bundle
mega_tasks.one_line_ask: Build the full release-art package for a tech-house single: a 1600x1600 streaming cover with a duotone + halftone + film-grain creative treatment matched to the song, a set of artist promo photos graded to a moody club look with a single colour-splash accent, a vectorized monochrome logo lockup, and a batch of Illustrator-merged tour-date merch/sticker cards from a dates CSV.
deliverables: 1600x1600 streaming single cover (Apple Music/Spotify spec) with duotone + Ben-Day halftone + film grain creative FX matched to the track aesthetic
A set of artist promo photos graded to a consistent moody club look
A selective-colour / colour-splash treatment isolating one brand accent (e.g. magenta neon) on the promo photos
A vectorized monochrome logo lockup for cover + merch
A batch of tour-date merch / sticker cards data-merged from the dates CSV (Illustrator)
Square + story social crops of the cover for the release announce
iterative_chain: asset_finalize_file_upload.out(artist press photo) -> image_select_subject.in (mask the artist)
image_adjust_exposure.out -> image_adjust_dark_portions.in (crush blacks for club mood)
image_select_by_prompt.out(accent mask) -> image_adjust_single_color_saturation.in (boost just magenta inside mask)
image_invert_selection.out(mask) -> image_apply_monochromatic_tint.in (duotone everything but the accent = colour splash)
image_add_grain.out(final cover) -> image_crop_and_resize.in (1600 cover + square/story crops)

input_assets.what: Raw artist press / live performance photos to grade, splash and treat
Artist logo bitmap to vectorize into a monochrome lockup
Reference Catch&Release-style cover supplied by client (aesthetic anchor)
Tour/club dates + merch line CSV driving the data-merged merch/sticker cards
Illustrator (.ai) merch/sticker card template with variable date/venue fields

mega_tasks.title: 4-colour screen-print separation pack — split approved cloud-and-lettering tee artwork into a white underbase + lavender/cobalt/deep-purple spot plates with masked halftone dot fields, a dot-gain choke, a licensed grunge distress pass, a VHS-glitch alt colourway, a vectorized lettering plate, and a real Illustrator-rendered separation/registration sheet driven by a spot-colour CSV
mega_tasks.source: Upwork db-2919 'Halftone Conversion for Screen Printing' (4 colours on a black tee — white underbase, lavender, cobalt blue, deep purple; halftone the cloud gradients, keep hard edges on the lettering). Upgraded to the smoke-tested 2026-06-15 connector reality: per-spot plates built with masked image_apply_halftone (blackAndWhite per channel) + custom-RGB image_fill_area / image_apply_color_overlay; a genuine .ai produced by document_merge_data_vector then exported with document_render_vector; final proof pack composed locally (PIL).
mega_tasks.one_line_ask: Take the approved tee artwork (airbrushed cloud gradient + hard-edge slogan lettering on a black ground) and produce a print-ready 4-colour screen-print separation pack for a black tee — a white underbase plate, three masked-halftone spot plates (lavender, cobalt blue, deep purple) with a dot-gain choke, a licensed grunge distress pass and a VHS chromatic-aberration alt colourway for the merch drop, a vectorized lettering plate, and an Illustrator-rendered separation sheet plus a per-colour registration/swatch card set merged from the shop's spot-colour CSV.
deliverables: White underbase separation — the artwork selected off the black ground, inverted, and filled solid white (the plate the shop flashes first)

Three spot-colour halftone plates (lavender, cobalt blue, deep purple) — cloud gradients converted to black-and-white Ben-Day dot fields inside the cloud mask, lettering held out so it stays a hard-edge solid
A dot-gain choke pass (slight gaussian on the dot field) so the press doesn't plug the shadows on the black tee
Per-spot colour proofs — each plate tinted to its target Pantone build via custom-RGB monochromatic tint / colour overlay (multiply) so the client sees the colourway on black
A vectorized hard-edge lettering plate (clean SVG/AI) for a crisp top print
A distressed merch alt colourway — licensed grunge texture from Adobe Stock multiplied in, film grain + noise, and a VHS chromatic-aberration glitch
An Illustrator-rendered separation sheet (all four plates + registration marks) exported to print PDF, generated from a genuine merged .ai
A per-colour registration/swatch card set data-merged from the shop's spot-colour CSV (one card per ink: name, Pantone, mesh count, squeegee, flash temp)
A composed proof contact sheet (all plates + the two colourways) for client sign-off

input_assets.what: Approved tee artwork — airbrushed cloud-gradient illustration with hard-edge slogan lettering, on a flat black ground, ~4500px (gemini-3-pro-image NBP for the text-bearing lockup; gemini-3.1-flash-image for the cloud-gradient base)
Lettering-only and clouds-only source layers (two PNGs) so the lettering hold-out and cloud halftone can be verified independently
Three alt-drop layout frames / colourway reference plates for the merch run (sleeve hit, back print, pocket lockup)
Spot-colour CSV — one row per ink (ink_name, pantone, rgb_hex, mesh_count, squeegee_durometer, flash_temp_F, print_order) feeding the registration/swatch-card data merge
input_assets.generator: gemini-2.5-flash writer (CSV, 4 rows: white underbase, lavender, cobalt, deep purple)

input_assets.what: Print-spec / dot-gain note (JSON) — mesh, LPI target, dot-gain %, underbase strategy, max screen budget — to ground the halftone radius and choke
Authored Illustrator separation-sheet template (.ai) with named Variables for the per-colour registration/swatch cards (CLIENT-SUPPLIED, authored once in desktop Illustrator Variables panel)

workflow.note: Initialize upload of the approved tee artwork (cloud gradient + hard-edge lettering on black) into Adobe storage.
workflow.consumes: input asset: approved tee artwork PNG (~4500px)

workflow.note: Finalize the upload; returns the working presigned URL that seeds the whole separation pipeline.
Trim the artwork to the live print area (remove black margin) so screen budget and registration are computed on the true image extent.
Select the whole artwork off the flat black ground — produces the subject mask used to build the white underbase.
Invert so the artwork (not the black ground) is the fill target; this is the correct polarity for an underbase plate.
Fill the artwork region solid white (preset:white) to produce the WHITE UNDERBASE separation — the plate flashed first under the spot colours.
Mask 'the cloud gradient area' on the cropped artwork — isolates the soft tones that must become a halftone dot field.
Invert the cloud mask to derive the LETTERING hold-out region (everything that must stay a hard-edge solid, never dotted).

Apply a black-and-white Ben-Day halftone (blackAndWhite:true, radius from the dot-gain JSON) INSIDE the cloud mask only — lettering held out. This dot field is the shared geometry of the three spot plates.
Dot-gain CHOKE — a slight gaussian on the dot field (blurTarget:currentLayer) so dots don't plug/bridge in the press shadows on a black tee.
LAVENDER plate proof — tint the choked dot field to the lavender Pantone build (custom hue/saturation) so the client sees plate 1 on black.
Tune the COBALT-BLUE separation strength — push the blue band's saturation so the cobalt plate density reads correctly against lavender.
Shift the cobalt band hue precisely onto the target cobalt Pantone (HSL on the blue channel) — the press needs the exact ink, not 'a blue'.
DEEP-PURPLE plate proof — custom-RGB colour overlay in multiply over the dot field for the third spot plate.
Vectorize the held-out LETTERING region into a clean hard-edge vector plate (SVG/AI) — a crisp top-print plate with no dots.
workflow.consumes: step 8 (lettering hold-out region of the artwork)

workflow.note: Search Adobe Stock for a high-contrast grunge / distressed-paper texture (contentType photo, orientation square) for the alt-drop distress pass.
workflow.consumes: brief: merch alt colourway needs real grit

workflow.note: License + download the chosen grunge texture (full-res) before any edit — license-before-edit rule.
Multiply the licensed grunge texture over the deep-purple proof to knock the artwork back into a worn, on-garment look (start of the alt colourway).
Add film grain to the distressed base for a tactile, screened-on-fabric texture.
Add noise on top of the grain to break up flat ink areas — reads as a heavily-printed vintage tee.
VHS chromatic-aberration glitch — the signature distressed alt-colourway look for the merch drop.
Crop/resize the finished VHS alt colourway to the merch-mockup export size (e.g. 3600px print-on-demand front).
Recommend a heavy display face for the slogan / the separation-sheet headers — informs the lettering plate and the .ai sheet typography.

Merge the spot-colour CSV into the authored .ai (Variables) — one registration/swatch card per ink (name, Pantone, mesh, squeegee, flash temp). Produces a GENUINE multi-artboard .ai that doubles as the separation sheet.
workflow.consumes: input: authored .ai template + spot-colour CSV (informed by step 23 font)

workflow.note: Export the genuine merged .ai to a print PDF (all artboards) — the registration/swatch-card set AND the master separation sheet. Real .ai in, real PDF out.

Compose the final client proof: white underbase + 3 spot plates (steps 6/11/13/14) + vectorized lettering (15) + VHS alt colourway (22) + the rendered sep sheet (25) into one labelled contact sheet. The connector edits ELEMENTS; arbitrary multi-element layout is local.

iterative_chain: image_select_by_prompt.out(cloud mask) + image_crop_to_bounds.out -> image_apply_halftone.in (B&W dots, clouds only)

image_fill_area.out + image_apply_monochromatic_tint.out + image_adjust_hsl.out + image_apply_color_overlay.out + image_vectorize.out + image_crop_and_resize.out + document_render_vector.out -> [local PIL] contact sheet
templates_to_author: Illustrator separation-sheet template 'screenprint_sep_sheet.ai' (authored once in desktop Illustrator, Variables panel bound). Two artboard families: (A) MASTER SEPARATION SHEET artboard 1 — four labelled plate-placeholder frames (UNDERBASE / LAVENDER / COBALT / DEEP PURPLE) arranged 2x2, plus crop/registration target marks at all four corners and a centre bullseye; a title block bound to Variables {job_name}, {tee_colour}, {total_screens}, {date}. (B) PER-INK REGISTRATION/SWATCH CARDS artboards 2..N (one per CSV row) — a 3.5x2in card whose swatch rectangle fill is driven by Variable {rgb_hex} and text frames bound to Variables {ink_name}, {pantone}, {mesh_count}, {squeegee_durometer}, {flash_temp_F}, {print_order}. CSV column headers must match the Variable names EXACTLY: ink_name, pantone, rgb_hex, mesh_count, squeegee_durometer, flash_temp_F, print_order. 4 rows: 'White Underbase'/no Pantone/FFFFFF, 'Lavender'/2635C/.., 'Cobalt Blue'/2945C/.., 'Deep Purple'/2685C/.. .

mega_tasks.title: Conference attendee badge + certificate suite — 510 personalized A6 badges from a roster CSV + headshot folder, uniformly cropped/retouched faces on a shared backdrop, plus InDesign-merged completion certificates exported as print PDFs
mega_tasks.source: Freelancer 'event-badge-name-photo-placement' (510 badges, CMYK/300dpi/A6, supplied PSD+PDF master, headshot folder, name list) — scraped pool event_conference_kit
mega_tasks.one_line_ask: From a 510-row attendee roster CSV and a folder of mixed headshots, produce 510 print-ready A6 (CMYK, 300dpi) name badges via InDesign data-merge — every headshot first cropped/centred/retouched to a uniform look on a shared badge backdrop — then data-merge matching completion certificates from the same roster and export everything to print PDF.
deliverables: 510 print-ready A6 (CMYK, 300dpi) name badges, one per attendee, merged from the roster CSV with name + headshot
Every headshot cropped/centred/scaled uniformly and tonally matched (consistent exposure/contrast)
A uniform solid badge-backdrop colour behind each subject + one shared look preset
Matching completion/attendance certificates merged one-per-attendee from the same roster
InDesign layouts rendered and exported as print-ready PDFs (badges + certificates)
Recommended badge/certificate typography that reads cleanly at A6
iterative_chain: asset_finalize_file_upload.out(raw headshot) -> image_auto_straighten.in (level tilted phone shots)
image_auto_straighten.out -> image_crop_and_resize.in (fit:extract on face -> uniform centred crop)
image_invert_selection.out(bg mask) -> image_fill_area.in (shared solid badge-backdrop colour)
image_fill_area.out -> image_apply_preset.in (one shared look; name from image_list_presets)
image_apply_preset.out(retouched headshots) -> document_merge_data_layout.in (as additionalImageFiles for badge .indd + roster CSV)
font_recommend.out(badge type) -> document_merge_data_layout.in (font choice for the merge)
document_merge_data_layout.out(per-row badge) -> document_render_layout.in (render badge + certificate PDFs)
document_render_layout.out -> document_convert_pdf.in (round-trip a flattened proof PDF back to INDD for last-minute tweaks)

input_assets.what: Attendee headshots (one per badge, mixed framing/lighting) needing uniform crop + retouch — representative batch of the 510-photo set
Attendee roster CSV (name, organization, title, certificate number, photo filename) driving both merges
Branded badge master layout supplied by client (front + back) as InDesign .indd template
Completion-certificate .indd template
Backdrop colour swatch / brand hex behind each subject

mega_tasks.title: Real-estate lead-gen tour video + reels package — narrated property walkthrough cut from room clips, cleaned voiceover, key-segment summary, graded hero thumbnail, and 9:16/1:1 social resizes for Facebook Feed/Stories/Reels
mega_tasks.source: Freelancer db-366 'Lead-Generating Real Estate Video Reels' composited with db-1885 'Vibrant Social Media Reels Creation' and db-641 'Fun YouTube Podcast Editing' (VO cleanup) — full Premiere video/audio coverage
mega_tasks.one_line_ask: Turn room-by-room property clips + a rough agent voiceover into a lead-gen Facebook tour: summarize the footage to find the key spaces, build a tight quick-cut walkthrough, clean and isolate the VO from music/reverb, grade a hero thumbnail still, and export the tour in Facebook Feed (1:1), Stories and Reels (9:16) sizes.
deliverables: A narrated, tour-guide-style property walkthrough video cut from the room clips with hooks in the first 3 seconds
A cleaned voiceover track separated from background music/ambient and reverb
A key-segment summary/transcript of the footage used to choose which rooms lead
A graded hero thumbnail/cover still pulled and tonally finished for the listing
Facebook Feed (1:1), Stories (9:16) and Reels (9:16) resized exports, MP4/H.264
Consistent aesthetic across the cut for the lead-gen campaign
iterative_chain: asset_finalize_file_upload.out(room walkthrough clips) -> media_summarize.in (find key spaces / spoken beats)
asset_finalize_file_upload.out(agent VO+music clip) -> media_enhance_speech.in (split clean speech vs music vs reverb)
media_summarize.out(key-segment guidance) -> video_create_quick_cut.in (assemble tight tour from the strongest rooms)
media_enhance_speech.out(clean speech track) -> video_create_quick_cut.in (lay cleaned VO under the cut)
video_create_quick_cut.out(tour master) -> video_resize.in (1:1 Feed, then 9:16 Stories/Reels)
asset_finalize_file_upload.out(listing hero still) -> image_auto_straighten.in (level the frame)

input_assets.what: Room-by-room property walkthrough clips to summarize and cut into the tour
Agent voiceover narration recorded over a rough music bed (deliberately noisy/reverberant so media_enhance_speech has real audio to separate)
Royalty-free music bed for the final mix
Listing hero/exterior + twilight stills used for the graded thumbnail and covers

tools_human_or_unavailable: change_background_color (Express-only: requires a templateOrDocumentURN on an Adobe Express design — not headless; the solid-backdrop need is met instead by image_select_subject -> image_invert_selection -> image_fill_area, used in T1/T5/T6)
generative fill / inpainting (unavailable — no AI object add/remove)
background replacement by prompt (unavailable — only solid-color fill)
video trim-to-timestamp / format conversion (unavailable — only quick_cut highlight + resize)
notes:
7 mega-tasks, each 13-19 distinct connectors with heavy output->input chaining and 6-33 inputs across image/data/video/audio. COLLECTIVE COVERAGE of every headless-usable connector in the ~50-tool suite is COMPLETE (tools_uncovered_headless is empty).

Provenance: T1/T2/T3/T5 = db (verbatim DB anchors db-4478, db-6004, db-1847, db-2919, read in full from pipeline/data/adobe.db). T6 = scraped (Freelancer 510-badge brief from input pool 1). T4 = composite (real Freelancer Tech-House cover brief + real Upwork album-art-package brief, both cited). T7 = composite (db-366 real-estate reels + db-1885 vibrant reels + db-641 podcast VO cleanup, all real DB anchors).

KEY HONESTY CORRECTION verified against the live tool schema: change_background_color is NOT the product-on-grey/solid-backdrop tool — its schema requires `templateOrDocumentURN` and 'Only works on Express designs', so it is Express-bound/needs_human. The scraped pool's implied_connector_workflow used it as a backdrop tool, which is wrong. Every task that needs a clean solid background instead uses the correct 3-step recipe image_select_subject -> image_invert_selection -> image_fill_area (the image_fill_area schema explicitly documents this recipe). This is why image_fill_area appears in T1/T5/T6 and change_background_color appears nowhere.

Every tool call in each workflow is genuinely load-bearing: selection tools always precede the masked tonal/FX/fill tool that consumes their mask (correct mask polarity respected — subject mask then invert before background fill/blur); image_list_presets always precedes image_apply_preset (names must be fetched, not guessed); asset_search precedes asset_license_and_download_stock precedes any edit on stock (license-before-edit rule); font_recommend precedes the data-merge it informs; media_summarize/media_enhance_speech feed video_create_quick_cut which feeds video_resize. document_merge_data_* outputs feed document_render_* / document_convert_pdf.

Both data-merge variants are exercised with both render tools: VECTOR (Illustrator) merge+render in T2/T4 (and render_vector again in T5), LAYOUT (InDesign) merge+render+convert_pdf in T6. All four Premiere video/audio tools land in T7. All fine tonal adjusts including single_color_saturation (T4/T5), light_portions (T2), all six creative-FX tools (halftone/monochromatic_tint/color_overlay in T4+T5, glitch+noise in T5, grain in T4), masking pipeline, both blurs (lens T1/T3/T4, gaussian T3), auto_straighten, crop_to_bounds, generative_expand, vectorize, and stock search+license are all represented.
